---
title: "Spencer A. Hill"
subtitle: "Assistant Professor, Dept. of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences, The City College of New York"
pagetitle: "Spencer A. Hill"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/site_pic.jpeg
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:shill1@ccny.cuny.edu
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/spencerahill
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/spencerahill
    - icon: google
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=spencerahill
---

## Overview

Welcome! I am a climate scientist interested in tropical overturning
circulations ([monsoons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monsoon),
[Hadley cells](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hadley_cell), and the
[Intertropical Convergence Zone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intertropical_Convergence_Zone),
a.k.a. the ITCZ). Specifically:

- Why they exist when and where they do
- How they ebb and flow over the course of each year
- How well we can predict their variations from year to year
- How those year-to-year variations affect agricultural and economic outcomes
- How they may have changed in Earth's history
- How they may change in Earth's future
- How they behave in other planetary atmospheres.

I'm also interested in how weather and climate impact real-world outcomes for
New York City. I explore all this with the other talented members of my New
York Climate Investigations: Theory To Impacts (New York CITTI) research group.
Please see the [Publications](publications.qmd) page to learn more about our past
work, the [Group](group.qmd) page to learn more about who we are, and the
[Opportunities](opportunities.qmd) page to learn more about joining us.

I work in the [Department of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences](https://www.ccny.cuny.edu/eas)
at [The City College of New York](https://www.ccny.cuny.edu/) as an Assistant Professor.

I also hold an adjunct position at the [Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory](https://www.ldeo.columbia.edu/)
at [Columbia University](https://www.columbia.edu/), where I collaborate with
[Michela Biasutti](https://www.ldeo.columbia.edu/~biasutti/) on a project
investigating the role of energy transports by tropical transient disturbances
in setting the location of the ITCZ.

I am also interested in the connections between weather and climate
science on the one hand with music, literature, poetry, and other forms of human
expression on the other hand. I write about this at my
[blog](https://spencerahill.github.io/couldnt-stand-the-weather/home.html), and occasionally tweet about it from my account
[\@spencerahill](https://twitter.com/spencerahill) using the hashtag
[#CliSciHiFi](https://twitter.com/hashtag/CliSciHiFi).

I also co-founded the "[DIYnamics](https://diynamics.github.io/)" project that
seeks to improve the teaching of meteorology, climate science, and other
planetary-scale sciences at the K-12 through graduate school levels by
developing inexpensive, easy-to-use kits for performing physical demonstrations
of core scientific concepts. Please see the DIYnamics
[website](https://diynamics.github.io/) and
[YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCnUHxOSVY4G4OFbF8XL1qUg) for more.

Finally, I am also interested in developing software tools for scientists in
the Python programming language. Please see my [Resources](resources.qmd) page
for more.

Please contact me with any questions about my research or anything else you
find on this site. Thanks!

---

## Contact

[shill1@ccny.cuny.edu](mailto:shill1@ccny.cuny.edu) | Room 733 | Marshak Science
Building | The City College of New York | 160 Convent Avenue | New York, NY 10031
